title: K-Similar Strings
hints: Focus on positions where A differs from B. Each swap can fix at most two such positions, so you want to maximize the impact of every swap.
This is a shortest-path problem on a graph where nodes are string states. Breadth-First Search finds the minimum number of swaps efficiently.
A state is defined by the current string configuration. The base case is when the string matches B. Only swap characters that actually move the string closer to B.
